title: '@Scheduled' annotation should only be applied to no-arg methods
description: Ensures that the '@Scheduled' annotation in Spring is only applied to methods that do not take any arguments. Applying '@Scheduled' to methods with parameters can lead to unexpected behavior or runtime errors, as the scheduling infrastructure cannot provide arguments when invoking the method.
recommendation: Refactor any '@Scheduled' methods to have no parameters.
impact: Incorrectly annotated methods may not be executed as scheduled, or may cause application errors.

checks.message: Refactor any '@Scheduled' methods to have no parameters.
